activity:
13:30 – Reflective ATR-FTIR on Thin Films and Multilayer Structures  
They switch to Attenuated Total Reflectance (ATR) mode to analyze a 200-nm bilayer of polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) and polyethyleneimine (PEI) deposited via layer-by-layer assembly. Using a diamond ATR crystal, they acquire spectra at varying angles of incidence to probe depth-dependent chemical gradients. They observe a shift in the NH₂ bending mode (1550 cm⁻¹) at higher angles, indicating preferential orientation of PEI chains near the substrate—evidence of interfacial self-assembly. They log the findings in their electronic lab notebook with annotated spectral overlays.
prompt: What if the PEI chains aren’t just oriented—they’re crosslinked by trace aldehydes from PVA oxidation? Would that explain the rigidity of the signal shift across multiple scans?
